hero.subtitle: Architecture studio in Marbella. Residential design, urban planning and interior design since 2000 on the Costa del Sol.

items.title: Our philosophy
items.body: We believe in architecture that responds to place, client and time. Every project is an opportunity to create spaces that endure and inspire.

items.title: Our team
items.body: A multidisciplinary team of architects, interior designers and urban planners with over 25 years of experience on the Costa del Sol and international projects.

items.title: Our process
items.body: We work collaboratively with each client, from the first sketch to handover, ensuring quality and attention to detail at every stage.

services.subtitle: Comprehensive architecture, design and urban planning solutions for residential and commercial projects.

items.title: Residential Architecture
items.body: Design and construction of villas, apartments and single-family homes tailored to each client and environment.

items.title: Urban Planning
items.body: Urban planning, land use and mixed-use projects on the Costa del Sol.

items.title: Interior Design
items.body: High-end interior design for residential and commercial spaces using premium materials.

items.title: Commercial Architecture
items.body: Hotels, offices and workspaces designed to maximise user experience and efficiency.

contact.subtitle: We are available to assist you
contact.address:
Urb. La Capellania, Plot nº20

footer.tagline: Architecture studio in Marbella specialising in residential design, urban planning and interior design since 2000.

articles.title: New Public Library of San Pedro de Alcántara
articles.excerpt: MoMaA has been selected to design the new Municipal Library of San Pedro de Alcántara, a cultural space of over 1,300 m² conceived as a community hub with a focus on innovation and sustainability.

articles.title: Winner: Marbella City Hall Rehabilitation Competition
articles.excerpt: The studio won the public competition for the rehabilitation of the Marbella City Hall building at Plaza de los Naranjos, a project co-funded by the EU Next Generation funds.

articles.title: Sustainable Architecture on the Costa del Sol
articles.excerpt: Demand for sustainable and bioclimatic architecture on the Costa del Sol continues to grow. At MoMaA we have spent over 25 years integrating natural materials, energy efficiency and contextual design into every residential and public project.
